context:
<Profile>### IDENTITY
- **Name:** Kevin O'Brien  
- **Age:** 36  
- **Gender:** Male  
- **Experience level:** **Intermediate** — 3 years of consistent training; general fitness focus (strength + conditioning + skills), not sport-specific.

---

### GOALS
- **Primary goal(s):**
  - Build **strength and muscle** (general strength + hypertrophy focus)
  - Develop **well-rounded “ready for anything” fitness** (hiking, sports, physical challenges)
- **Secondary goals:**
  - Maintain/improve conditioning alongside strength
  - Variety-driven training (avoid overly narrow specialization)
- **Target events/deadlines:** None — ongoing development

---

## TRAINING CONTEXT

### Schedule & Availability
- **Available training days:** **Mon / Wed / Fri / Sat**
- **Desired training frequency:** **4x/week** (matches available days)
- **Session duration:** **60 minutes**
- **Time windows:** **Noon** on training days
- **Blocked days:** Tue/Thu/Sun (not specified; assumed unavailable)

### Equipment & Environment
- **Primary location:** Commercial gym — **Crunch Fitness**
- **Available equipment:** Full commercial setup including **barbells, dumbbells, machines, cables** (assume standard racks/benches/platforms typical of commercial gyms)

### Constraints
- **[ACTIVE]** None reported (as of 2026-02-27)
- **[RESOLVED]** None reported

### Preferences
- **Training style:** Likes **variety**; enjoys a **mix of strength, conditioning, and skills**
- **Focus areas:** General athleticism / “jack of all trades” preparedness

---

## METRICS
(As of **2026-02-27** unless otherwise noted; dates not provided for individual performances, so recorded as “as of” today.)

### Strength
- **Back Squat:** **245 lb x 5** (as of 2026-02-27)
- **Bench Press:** **205 lb x 5** (as of 2026-02-27)
- **Deadlift:** **315 lb x 5** (as of 2026-02-27)

### Endurance
- **5K:** **25:00** (as of 2026-02-27)

### Movement Quality
- No movement-quality assessments or pain notes provided (as of 2026-02-27)

### Body Composition
- No bodyweight/body fat data provided (as of 2026-02-27)

---

## LOG
## 2026-02-27 — Profile created/updated (intake)
- Set primary goals: build strength and muscle; maintain well-rounded general fitness with variety.
- Schedule confirmed: **Mon/Wed/Fri/Sat**, **noon**, **60 min**, target **4 sessions/week**.
- Equipment confirmed: **Crunch Fitness commercial gym** (barbells, DBs, machines, cables).
- Recorded baseline metrics (as of 2026-02-27): Squat 245x5, Bench 205x5, Deadlift 315x5, 5K 25:00.
- Constraints: none reported.

If you want tighter validation, share: current bodyweight, height, any recurring aches/injuries, and whether the listed lifts are high-bar/low-bar squat, touch-and-go or paused bench, and deadlift style (conventional/sumo).</Profile>

reference.response:
## Header
- **Program name:** Jack-of-All-Trades Strength & Hypertrophy (with Built-In Conditioning)
- **Program Owner:** GymText
- **User:** Kevin O’Brien
- **Goal:** Build strength and muscle while staying “ready for anything” (balanced athletic fitness + conditioning)
- **Duration:** **Ongoing with phase cycling**

---

## Program Philosophy
Kevin is an intermediate lifter with solid baseline strength and a clear preference for variety and well-rounded preparedness. A **4-day upper/lower split (Mon/Wed/Fri/Sat)** fits his schedule and allows frequent exposure to big lifts while keeping sessions inside **60 minutes** by controlling total working sets and using targeted supersets. The plan blends **strength-first compounds** (to drive progression) with **hypertrophy accessories** (to build muscle and resilience) and **short, structured conditioning finishers** (to maintain/improve the 5K base without stealing recovery). Progression is primarily **RPE- and rep-target driven** (autoregulated for day-to-day readiness) with planned **deloads every 4th week** and rotating secondary variations each phase to satisfy variety while still progressing the main patterns.

---

# Phase 1: Base & Volume (Accumulation) — Weeks 1–4
**Intent:** Build work capacity, add muscle, clean up movement quality, and set up the next strength push.  
**Intensity bias:** Mostly **RPE 6–8**, moderate loads, higher total sets.

## Weekly Pattern

### **Mon — Upper Strength + Pull Volume**
- **Focus:** Bench strength + heavy rows; shoulder balance
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets
- **Bench Press (touch-and-go):** 4 × 4–6 @ **RPE 7**
- **Weighted Pull-Up** *(or heavy lat pulldown if needed)*: 4 × 4–6 @ **RPE 7** — *big vertical pull to balance pressing and build “usable” upper strength*
- **Incline DB Press:** 3 × 8–10 @ **RPE 8**
- **Chest-Supported Row (machine/DB):** 3 × 8–10 @ **RPE 8**
- **Cable Lateral Raise:** 2 × 12–20 @ **RPE 8**
- **Optional 6-minute finisher (if time):** Row erg 6 min @ steady **Zone 2–3**

---

### **Wed — Lower Strength + Carries**
- **Focus:** Squat strength + hinge exposure; trunk and grip
- **Volume:** ~14–17 working sets
- **Back Squat:** 4 × 4–6 @ **RPE 7**
- **Romanian Deadlift (barbell or DB):** 3 × 6–8 @ **RPE 7–8** — *posterior chain strength without frying recovery like frequent heavy deadlifts*
- **Walking Lunge (DB):** 3 × 8–12 each leg @ **RPE 8** — *single-leg resilience for hiking/sports*
- **Leg Curl (machine):** 2 × 10–15 @ **RPE 8**
- **Farmer Carry (heavy DB/handles):** 6 × 30–40 m (rest as needed) — *grip + trunk + “real-world” work capacity*

---

### **Fri — Upper Hypertrophy + Short Intervals**
- **Focus:** Upper volume, arms/shoulders, athletic conditioning
- **Volume:** ~16–20 working sets
- **Overhead Press (barbell or DB):** 3 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**
- **One-Arm Cable Row:** 3 × 8–12 each @ **RPE 8**
- **Dips** *(assisted if needed)* **or** **Machine Chest Press:** 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 8**
- **Lat Pulldown (different grip than Monday):** 3 × 10–12 @ **RPE 8**
- **Superset (time-saver):**
  - **EZ-Bar Curl:** 2–3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 8**
  - **Rope Pressdown:** 2–3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 8**
- **Conditioning finisher (8–10 minutes):**  
  Bike or row: **10 rounds** of **:20 hard / :40 easy** — *keeps conditioning progressing without long sessions*

---

### **Sat — Lower Hypertrophy + Power/Skills**
- **Focus:** Deadlift pattern + legs; basic power and trunk
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets
- **Deadlift (conventional or sumo—your usual style):** 3 × 4–6 @ **RPE 7** — *practice + strength without maxing*
- **Front Squat** *(or hack squat/leg press if you prefer)*: 3 × 8–10 @ **RPE 8**
- **Hip Thrust (barbell or machine):** 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 8**
- **Calf Raise:** 2 × 10–20 @ **RPE 8**
- **Hanging Knee Raise** *(or ab wheel)*: 3 × 8–15 @ **RPE 8**
- **Skill/power primer (keep crisp, not fatiguing):**
  - **Box Jump:** 5 × 2 (full reset) — *rate of force for “athletic” readiness*

---

## Progression Strategy (Phase 1)
- **Main lifts (bench/squat/deadlift):** Use a **rep-range progression**.
  - Example: 4×4–6 @ RPE 7. Start Week 1 at 4 reps; add reps week-to-week until you hit the top of the range with solid bar speed. Then add **+5 lb (upper)** / **+10 lb (lower)** next week and repeat.
- **Accessories:** **Double progression**
  - Add reps within the range first (e.g., 8→12), then add load and restart near the low end.
- **Conditioning:** Keep the same interval structure; try to slightly increase output (watts/pace) or reduce perceived effort at the same output.
- **Deload (Week 4):**
  - Reduce **volume ~40–50%** (fewer sets), keep intensity moderate (**~RPE 6–7**). Keep conditioning easy (Zone 2 only, 10–20 min).

**Transition to Phase 2 when:** Week 4 deload is complete and you feel recovered (sleep, joints, motivation stable).

---

# Phase 2: Strength Emphasis (Intensification) — Weeks 5–8
**Intent:** Push strength up while maintaining muscle and conditioning.  
**Intensity bias:** More work at **RPE 7–9**, slightly lower total sets than Phase 1.

## Weekly Pattern

### **Mon — Upper Heavy (Bench Priority)**
- **Focus:** Heavier bench + upper back strength
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets
- **Bench Press:** 5 × 3–5 @ **RPE 8**
- **Paused Bench (1-sec pause):** 2 × 4–6 @ **RPE 7** — *tightness and control off the chest*
- **Weighted Pull-Up:** 4 × 3–5 @ **RPE 8**
- **DB Row (heavy):** 3 × 6–10 @ **RPE 8**
- **Face Pull:** 2 × 12–20 @ **RPE 8**

---

### **Wed — Lower Heavy (Squat Priority)**
- **Focus:** Heavier squat + bracing
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets
- **Back Squat:** 5 × 3–5 @ **RPE 8**
- **Tempo RDL (3-sec down):** 3 × 6 @ **RPE 7–8** — *positions + hamstrings without chasing max load*
- **Split Squat:** 2–3 × 8–10 each @ **RPE 8**
- **Pallof Press:** 3 × 10–15 each side

---

### **Fri — Upper Volume + Aerobic Base**
- **Focus:** Hypertrophy and shoulder health; easy conditioning
- **Volume:** ~16–20 working sets
- **Overhead Press:** 4 × 6–8 @ **RPE 7–8**
- **Seated Cable Row:** 4 × 8–12 @ **RPE 8**
- **Incline DB Press:** 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 8**
- **Lat Pulldown:** 3 × 10–12 @ **RPE 8**
- **Lateral Raise:** 2 × 12–20 @ **RPE 8**
- **Zone 2 (10–15 minutes):** incline walk/bike/row @ conversational pace

---

### **Sat — Lower Pull + “Work Capacity”**
- **Focus:** Deadlift strength + athletic engine
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets + finisher
- **Deadlift:** 5 × 2–4 @ **RPE 8**
- **Leg Press:** 3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 8**
- **Hamstring Curl:** 2–3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 8**
- **Loaded Carry Medley (8 minutes):** rotate as space allows  
  - suitcase carry / farmer carry / sled push (if available) — *real-world conditioning without needing a track*

---

## Progression Strategy (Phase 2)
- **Main lifts:** Add load in small jumps week-to-week while keeping top sets in target RPE.
  - If you overshoot (RPE 9+ too early), keep load the same next week and add a rep or reduce one set.
- **Variations (paused bench, tempo RDL):** Progress slowly—these are **quality builders**, not PR chases.
- **Deload (Week 8):** Same approach as Phase 1 (cut sets ~40–50%, keep technique sharp).

**Transition to Phase 3 when:** Heavy work feels slower/jointy or motivation dips—Phase 3 refreshes variety and athletic output.

---

# Phase 3: Athletic Hypertrophy + Conditioning (Undulating) — Weeks 9–12
**Intent:** Keep strength, add variety/athleticism, and bump conditioning while still growing muscle.  
**Intensity bias:** Mix of **moderate strength** + **higher-rep hypertrophy** + **conditioning**.

## Weekly Pattern

### **Mon — Upper Power + Strength**
- **Focus:** Fast intent + solid strength
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets
- **Med Ball Chest Pass:** 6 × 3 (full reset) — *upper power without joint stress*
- **Bench Press:** 4 × 4 @ **RPE 7–8**
- **Barbell or Machine Row:** 4 × 6–8 @ **RPE 8**
- **DB Incline Press:** 3 × 10–12 @ **RPE 8**
- **Rear Delt Fly:** 2 × 15–25 @ **RPE 8**

---

### **Wed — Lower Strength (Variation)**
- **Focus:** Knee/hip strength via variation for novelty and resilience
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets
- **Front Squat** *(or safety bar squat if available)*: 4 × 4–6 @ **RPE 7–8** — *variation drives new stimulus while giving the back squat a break*
- **Trap Bar Deadlift** *(or block pull if no trap bar)*: 3 × 4–6 @ **RPE 7–8**
- **Step-Up:** 3 × 8–12 each @ **RPE 8**
- **Cable Woodchop:** 3 × 10–15 each side

---

### **Fri — Upper Pump + Intervals**
- **Focus:** High-quality volume; arms/shoulders; harder conditioning
- **Volume:** ~16–22 working sets
- **Seated DB Shoulder Press:** 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 8**
- **Pull-Ups (bodyweight):** 4 × AMRAP leaving **1–2 reps in reserve**
- **Cable Fly:** 3 × 12–15 @ **RPE 8**
- **Chest-Supported Row:** 3 × 10–12 @ **RPE 8**
- **Superset:**
  - **Cable Curl:** 3 × 12–15 @ **RPE 8**
  - **Overhead Triceps Extension:** 3 × 12–15 @ **RPE 8**
- **Intervals (10 minutes):** 5 rounds of **1:00 hard / 1:00 easy**

---

### **Sat — Lower Hypertrophy + “Chassis”**
- **Focus:** Legs/glutes + trunk/conditioning blend
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets
- **Deadlift (lighter, speed focus):** 6 × 2 @ **~RPE 6–7** (move fast, perfect reps)
- **Hack Squat or Leg Press:** 4 × 10–15 @ **RPE 8**
- **Hip Thrust:** 3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 8**
- **Sled Push** *(if available)*: 6–10 pushes of 15–25 m, moderate-hard — *excellent “ready for anything” conditioning with low soreness cost*
- **Plank Variation:** 3 × 30–60 sec

---

## Progression Strategy (Phase 3)
- **Strength slots (bench/front squat):** Small load or rep increases weekly while staying **RPE 7–8**.
- **Hypertrophy work:** Add reps first, then load; keep rest times honest (60–90 sec on many accessories) to fit the 60-minute cap.
- **Conditioning:** Increase density (same time, slightly more work) or keep output constant while lowering perceived effort.
- **Deload (Week 12):** Reduce sets ~50%, keep 1 short easy Zone 2 session only.

---

## Phase Cycling
**Default cycle:** **Phase 1 → Phase 2 → Phase 3 → repeat** (12-week macrocycle).
- Each repeat, keep the same structure but rotate **secondary variations** (not the whole program):
  - Swap incline DB press ↔ machine press, RDL ↔ good morning (light), step-ups ↔ reverse lunges, different row/pulldown grips, etc.
- **Triggers to extend a phase by 1–2 weeks:** Great recovery, steady progress, high motivation.
- **Triggers to switch phases early:** Joint irritation, stalled bar speed for 2 straight weeks, accumulating fatigue, or schedule stress.

---

## Modification History
- **2026-02-27:** Initial program created from intake profile (no constraints; 4 days/week; strength + hypertrophy + conditioning + variety emphasis).
